column1: # Vat.gr.1291, f.7r — Ptolemy, Handy Tables: NEW TABLE TYPE (likely parallax or related)
# Header: ΠΑΡΑΛΛΑΞΕΙΣ ΤΟΥ ΔΙΑ ΒΥΖΑΝΤΙΟΥ ΠΑΡΑΛΛΗΛΟΥ
# STRUCTURE: 6 small sub-tables per page (3 wide × 2 tall), one per zodiac sign
# Each sub-table is headed by zodiac sign + parameter "ὧ X̄ Y̅" (hour value)
# Columns: ω̅ (hour) | M̅ μηκ (μῆκος, length?) | ἡ̄ | πέρ (πέρας?)
# 12 rows per sub-table (one per hour of the day, α-ιβ)
#   TOP ROW: ΚΑΡΚΗΝΟΥ ὧ ζ νζ  |  ΛΕΟΝΤΟΣ ὧ ζ κβ  |  ΠΑΡΘΕΝΟΥ (partial, image 4)
#   BOTTOM ROW: ΖΥΓΟΥ ὧ ϛ  |  ΣΚΟΡΠΙΟΥ ὧ ε ιβ  |  ΤΟΞΟΤΟΥ ὧ δ κη
# I am NOT certain what astronomical quantity this table computes.
# The header word "παράλλαξις" suggests parallax, but the layout differs from
# standard parallax tables (which typically use double-entry with anomaly arguments).
# Without comparison to Tihon's critical edition I'm transcribing values but flagging
# Many cells contain uncertain readings - this page has fainter ink and smaller cells
# === ΠΑΡΘΕΝΟΥ — partially visible on right edge of image 4 ===
# Not transcribed - need clearer image of right side
# This page is structurally DIFFERENT from the ascension tables:
# 1. SIX small sub-tables per folio instead of THREE large ones
# 2. Each sub-table has its OWN header parameter (the "ὧ X Y" value)
# 3. Values are DISCRETE (not cumulative) — no monotonicity to validate against
# 4. The header pattern "ὧ ζ νζ" decreases through the zodiac:
#    This decreasing pattern suggests a SEASONAL parameter (perhaps day-length deficit
#    from 6 equinoctial hours, related to solar declination)
# Treat this as a SEPARATE PAGE CLASS for ML:
#   - Per-sub-table header parsing (sign name + sexagesimal parameter)
#   - Non-monotonic value validation (cross-reference between adjacent hours)
#   - col_M values often peak around hour 4-5, suggesting solar/lunar maximum at noon-adjacent hours
#   - col_per often has a near-discontinuity around hour 6-7 (parallax zero-crossing?)
#   - Last hour (ιβ) often resembles first hour with adjustment
